cves.name: EternalBlue SMBv1 Remote Code Execution
cves.description: The SMBv1 server in Microsoft Windows allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via crafted packets (MS17-010). Used by WannaCry and NotPetya ransomware.
cves.recommendation: Apply MS17-010 patch. Disable SMBv1 protocol.

cves.name: EternalRomance SMBv1 Remote Code Execution
cves.description: The SMBv1 server in Microsoft Windows allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via crafted packets (MS17-010).
cves.recommendation: Apply MS17-010 patch. Disable SMBv1 protocol.

cves.name: BlueKeep RDP Remote Code Execution
cves.description: A remote code execution vulnerability exists in Remote Desktop Services when an unauthenticated attacker sends specially crafted requests (pre-authentication, no user interaction).
cves.recommendation: Apply KB4499175. Enable Network Level Authentication. Disable RDP if not needed.

cves.name: Zerologon Netlogon Elevation of Privilege
cves.description: An elevation of privilege vulnerability exists when an attacker establishes a vulnerable Netlogon secure channel connection to a domain controller using the Netlogon Remote Protocol (MS-NRPC). An attacker can gain domain admin privileges.
cves.recommendation: Apply KB4571702. Enable enforcement mode for Netlogon secure channel connections.

cves.name: PrintNightmare Print Spooler RCE (Variant 1)
cves.description: Windows Print Spooler remote code execution vulnerability allows authenticated attackers to execute code with SYSTEM privileges.
cves.recommendation: Apply KB5003690. Disable Print Spooler service if not needed.

cves.name: PrintNightmare Print Spooler RCE (Variant 2)
cves.description: Windows Print Spooler remote code execution vulnerability. Authenticated attacker can run arbitrary code with SYSTEM privileges by installing a malicious printer driver.
cves.recommendation: Apply KB5004945. Disable Print Spooler service or restrict Point and Print.

cves.name: HiveNightmare / SeriousSAM Elevation of Privilege
cves.description: Windows elevation of privilege vulnerability due to overly permissive ACLs on SAM, SYSTEM, and SECURITY registry hive shadow copies, allowing local privilege escalation.
cves.recommendation: Apply KB5004605. Delete Volume Shadow Copies. Restrict access to %windir%\system32\config.

cves.name: Follina MSDT Remote Code Execution
cves.description: A remote code execution vulnerability exists when MSDT is called using the URL protocol from a calling application such as Word, allowing arbitrary code execution.
cves.recommendation: Apply June 2022 cumulative update. Disable MSDT URL protocol via registry.

cves.name: Windows TCP/IP IPv6 Remote Code Execution
cves.description: An unauthenticated attacker could repeatedly send IPv6 packets with specially crafted extensions to a Windows machine which could enable remote code execution.
cves.recommendation: Apply August 2024 cumulative update. Disable IPv6 if not required.

cves.name: Microsoft Outlook NTLM Credential Theft
cves.description: Elevation of privilege vulnerability in Microsoft Outlook. Attacker sends a specially crafted email triggering NTLM credential relay without user interaction.
cves.recommendation: Apply March 2023 security update. Block outbound TCP 445/SMB at the network perimeter.

cves.name: CurveBall Windows CryptoAPI Spoofing
cves.description: A spoofing vulnerability exists in the way Windows CryptoAPI (Crypt32.dll) validates Elliptic Curve Cryptography (ECC) certificates, allowing code signing and TLS certificate spoofing.

cves.name: SMBGhost SMBv3 Remote Code Execution
cves.description: A remote code execution vulnerability exists in the way Microsoft Server Message Block 3.1.1 (SMBv3) protocol handles certain requests due to buffer overflow in compression.

cves.name: HTTP Protocol Stack Remote Code Execution
cves.description: An unauthenticated attacker could send a specially crafted packet to a targeted server utilizing the HTTP Protocol Stack (http.sys) to process packets, enabling wormable RCE.

cves.name: Win32k Elevation of Privilege (MysterySnail)
cves.description: Win32k elevation of privilege vulnerability actively exploited in targeted attacks. Use-after-free in NtGdiResetDC allows kernel code execution.

cves.name: Win32k Elevation of Privilege
cves.description: Win32k elevation of privilege vulnerability due to type confusion. Local authenticated attacker can gain SYSTEM privileges.

cves.name: Windows TCP/IP Remote Code Execution
cves.description: An unauthenticated attacker could send a specially crafted IPv6 packet to a Windows node where IPSec is enabled, enabling remote code execution.

cves.name: Windows Graphics Component RCE
cves.description: Windows Graphics Component remote code execution vulnerability. Exploitation requires local access but can lead to SYSTEM-level code execution.

cves.name: Windows CLFS Driver Elevation of Privilege
cves.description: Windows Common Log File System (CLFS) driver elevation of privilege vulnerability actively exploited by Nokoyawa ransomware.

cves.name: Windows Search RCE via Office Documents
cves.description: Windows Search remote code execution vulnerability exploited via specially crafted Microsoft Office documents to bypass Mark of the Web protections.
cves.recommendation: Apply July 2023 cumulative update. Block Office from creating child processes via ASR rules.

cves.name: Windows Kernel Elevation of Privilege (AppLocker)
cves.description: Windows kernel elevation of privilege vulnerability in the appid.sys driver (AppLocker). Exploited by Lazarus Group for kernel read/write primitive.

cves.name: Windows Kernel Elevation of Privilege (TOCTOU)
cves.description: Windows kernel elevation of privilege vulnerability due to a race condition. Exploited by OilRig/APT34 to gain SYSTEM privileges.

cves.name: Microsoft Exchange Server Arbitrary File Write (ProxyLogon chain)
cves.description: A post-authentication arbitrary file write vulnerability in Microsoft Exchange Server, part of the ProxyLogon attack chain exploited by HAFNIUM.

cves.name: ProxyNotShell Exchange Server SSRF
cves.description: Microsoft Exchange Server server-side request forgery vulnerability. Used in combination with CVE-2022-41082 for authenticated remote code execution.

cves.name: ProxyNotShell Exchange Server RCE
cves.description: Microsoft Exchange Server remote code execution vulnerability via PowerShell deserialization. Chained with CVE-2022-41040 for exploitation.

cves.name: ProxyLogon Exchange Server SSRF
cves.description: Server-side request forgery vulnerability in Microsoft Exchange Server allowing unauthenticated attacker to send arbitrary HTTP requests and authenticate as the Exchange server. Exploited en masse by HAFNIUM and others.

cves.name: ProxyShell Exchange Server Pre-Auth Path Confusion
cves.description: Microsoft Exchange Server pre-authentication path confusion vulnerability. Part of the ProxyShell attack chain enabling unauthenticated RCE.
cves.recommendation: Apply April 2021 Exchange security update KB5001779.

cves.name: ProxyShell Exchange Server Elevation of Privilege
cves.description: Microsoft Exchange Server elevation of privilege vulnerability in the PowerShell backend. Part of the ProxyShell chain.
cves.recommendation: Apply April 2021 Exchange security update KB5001779.

cves.name: ProxyShell Exchange Server Post-Auth Arbitrary File Write
cves.description: Microsoft Exchange Server security feature bypass vulnerability. Post-authentication arbitrary file write completing the ProxyShell RCE chain.

cves.name: Exchange Server Unified Messaging Deserialization RCE
cves.description: Insecure deserialization vulnerability in the Unified Messaging service of Microsoft Exchange Server, part of the ProxyLogon attack chain.

cves.name: SIGRed Windows DNS Server RCE
cves.description: A wormable remote code execution vulnerability in Windows DNS Server. An unauthenticated attacker can send malicious DNS responses to exploit a heap-based buffer overflow.
cves.recommendation: Apply KB4565529. Implement workaround: limit DNS message size via registry.

cves.name: Active Directory Domain Services Elevation of Privilege (Certifried)
cves.description: An authenticated attacker could manipulate attributes on computer accounts they own and acquire a certificate from AD Certificate Services to escalate to domain admin.

cves.name: PetitPotam NTLM Relay via LSARPC
cves.description: Windows LSA spoofing vulnerability. An unauthenticated attacker can coerce domain controllers into authenticating to attacker-controlled servers via NTLM relay (PetitPotam).
cves.recommendation: Apply August 2021 cumulative update. Enable EPA on AD CS and disable NTLM where possible.

cves.name: Microsoft WordPad Information Disclosure
cves.description: Microsoft WordPad information disclosure vulnerability enabling NTLM hash theft. WordPad subsequently deprecated by Microsoft.

cves.name: Windows CLFS Driver Heap Overflow EoP
cves.description: Windows Common Log File System driver elevation of privilege vulnerability via heap-based buffer overflow. Actively exploited in the wild.

cves.name: Microsoft SharePoint Server Elevation of Privilege
cves.description: An attacker who gains access to spoofed JWT authentication tokens can use them to execute a network attack bypassing authentication and gaining administrator access to SharePoint Server.

cves.name: Windows SmartScreen Security Feature Bypass
cves.description: Internet Shortcut Files (.url) security feature bypass allowing attacker to bypass SmartScreen checks via specially crafted files. Exploited by DarkCasino/Water Hydra APT.

cves.name: Windows CLFS Driver Elevation of Privilege
cves.description: Windows Common Log File System driver elevation of privilege vulnerability. Exploited in the wild by multiple threat actors.

cves.name: Windows SmartScreen Security Feature Bypass (Magniber)
cves.description: Windows SmartScreen security feature bypass vulnerability. Exploited by Magniber ransomware using crafted MSI files to bypass Mark of the Web warnings.

cves.name: NTLM Hash Disclosure Spoofing Vulnerability
cves.description: NTLM Hash Disclosure spoofing vulnerability. Minimal user interaction with a malicious file (selecting, right-clicking, or performing any action other than opening) can trigger NTLMv2 hash disclosure.
